name: thc-pptp-bruter
title: thc-pptp-bruter

commands.name: thc-pptp-bruter
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# thc-pptp-bruter -h
 thc-pptp-bruter [options] <remote host IP>
 -v        Verbose output / Debug output
 -W        Disable windows hack [default: enabled]
 -u <user> User [default: administrator]
 -w <file> Wordlist file [default: stdin]
 -p <n>    PPTP port [default: 1723]
 -n <n>    Number of parallel tries [default: 5]
 -l <n>    Limit to n passwords / sec [default: 100]
 
 Windows-Hack reuses the LCP connection with the same caller-id. This
 gets around MS's anti-brute forcing protection. It's enabled by default.

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=e0260083c90d93620d1c2aba60a429acf8b9170f, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
THC PPTP Brute Force
Brute force program against pptp vpn endpoints (tcp port 1723).  Fully
standalone. Supports latest MSChapV2 authentication. Tested against Windows
and Cisco gateways.  Exploits a weakness in Microsoft's anti-brute force
implementation which makes it possible to try 300 passwords the second.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use thc-pptp-bruter because ### thc-pptp-bruter.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair thc-pptp-bruter with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `thc-pptp-bruter --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### thc-pptp-bruter.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install thc-pptp-bruter
